PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar May-23-2023 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-23-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Reed appearing via zoom for Kim Kouk Chow (Petitioner).

Mr. Reed stated to the court that a dismissal was filed, that he had calendaring issues and he couldn't make it to the April 25, hearing. The Court issues sanctions in the amount of $250.00 against Thomas Reed. Pursuant to CCP 177.5 payable within 10 days of the order.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Apr-25-2023 Continued To May-23-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to petitioner Kim Kouk Chow and to attorney Thomas B Reed to appear on May 23, 2023, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against each party for failure to appear. The petition for spousal property is also being continued to May 23, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Apr-20-2023 Continued To Apr-25-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Nov-30-2022 Continued To Apr-20-23 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Thomas B. Reed Jr., Esq. for Kim Kouk Chow (Petitioner). No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Mr. Reed stated that they are here to confirm that the two properties are the decedant's sole and separate properties. The Court stated that a true spousal property petition assumes that the property is community property. The Court noted that Ms. Ly died intestate. Mr. Reed briefly addressed the properties noting that the house in Oakland was acquired with the decedant's sister prior to marriage, that the decedant held a 50% interest in the property as tenants in common, that Irene Ly later married, that three years later the Richmond property was conveyed to Irene Ly via a quitclaim deed, and because Irene Ly died intestate that her mother would be entitled to 50%. The Court stated that due to the way that the petition is presented now that it can't be granted. Mr. Reed stated that they will probate the estate.

The matter is continued to April 20, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for the petitioner to file a petition for probate.

Judge: Ceclia Castellanos. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Lozado. Matter not reported.
